title: Setaria bongaensis
intro:
There is not enough information available on Setaria bongaensis. However, I can provide information on Setaria, a genus of plants. Setaria is a type of grass, specifically a genus of warm-season grasses in the family Poaceae. 

Setaria viridis, another species of Setaria, has some health benefits and bioactive constituents. 

Setaria viridis has been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antidiabetic properties. The bioactive constituents of Setaria viridis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids.

The most common herbal preparations made with Setaria viridis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions such as diabetes, inflammation, and oxidative stress.

Please note that the information provided is for Setaria viridis, not Setaria bongaensis, as there is not enough information available on the latter.
taxonomy: Setaria bongaensis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Setaria, which is a group of grasses commonly known as bristle grasses or foxtail grasses.
